title: Living in Boulder vlog 4 - 30 - 2017: Roman Timeline & Timechart

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs about a long overnight work session building a Roman timeline and timechart project. He troubleshot column/formatting issues caused by styled text that had to be stripped through Notepad, fixed navigation links between horizontal and vertical sections of the timeline, and attached YouTube icons and phone links to a timechart he plans to use as a show playlist. He outlines next steps: cleaning up Wikipedia-sourced text and linking dates from his 'living' pages back to the timeline.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: styled text from an external source caused column layout to break; stripping it through Notepad fixed it
happy with progress attaching YouTube icons and phone links; excited about linking it to the timeline

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He got up at 6 AM after working until 4 AM the previous day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He worked until noon and then slept for a couple of hours because he couldn't keep his eyes open.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He had to rebuild the Roman timeline because the columns were not working correctly.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The culprit for the timeline issue was just text formatting problems.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He had to paste the text into a notepad program and then back into the timeline to fix the formatting.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He fixed the links in the timeline so that clicking on a king's name takes you to the corresponding vertical part.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He worked on turning the time chart into a playlist for his show.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He attached YouTube icons to the back of the time chart.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He attached phone links to the dates in the timeline for easy navigation during his show.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to refine the timeline by cleaning up the text and making it look better than a simple Wikipedia copy-paste job.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wants to link the dates written in his living documents to the timeline for better integration.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He finds the prospect of completing these tasks super exciting.

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder vlog for April 30th, 2017. Right now it's May 1st, 2.53 a.m.
So yesterday I got up at, yeah, yeah, I was working until like 4 in the morning yesterday.
And then I got up at, was this today? And then I got up at like 6.00 because I just couldn't sleep anymore.
And then I worked until like noon and then I slept for like a couple hours during the day because I couldn't keep my eyes open.
Because apparently I didn't get a full night's sleep, even though I got up at 6.00 like I was fresh.
But then I worked until now and now it's 2.54 a.m.
So yesterday, at the beginning of the day, I was, the Roman timeline was totally a mess.
Because the columns were worked and I didn't know why.
So I had to rebuild it until I pinpointed the culprit, one of the many culprits, but the culprit was just text.
And I didn't, you know, I was looking at the HTML part and the raw code part and it was just words.
So I had to paste it onto like a text program notebook and then paste it back on the Dreamweaver
For it to not have whatever it had to allow it to snap to the correct size.
I had to do that with every single thing.
So that took a while.
And then, and so that took away the links of it.
But that's fine because now I can use the colors that I set for it.
But I was doing that all day.
And then fixing the links, I fixed the links too, which I want to do today.
And I finished it where you click on the horizontal and the vertical part of the links now.
So if the kings and the dynasties and the kingdoms, so when you click on a king, like on the horizontal part,
It'll take you to the vertical part and you click it and you turn it back up.
So that might be easy to do with the rest of the timeline.
I was talking and the recording suddenly stopped.
So maybe I'll just have a few minutes, seconds to talk here.
Maybe the memory's screwed up.
I have to restart the computer.
But what was I talking about?
Yeah, that's what I did.
And then after that, I went and I started working on the time chart,
Which I'm going to turn into the playlist for the show.
And I attached, it was like $1,500, $7,500.
And I attached all of the, because it's 365 days times 20.
I attached all of the YouTube icons to the back of it, the back side of it.
And even though I didn't have to, because it's not going to be ready until, you know, 20 years later.
But I was almost done.
I was like, wait, why am I doing this?
I just wanted it to look nice.
And then after that, I was attaching the links so that, for the dates,
So I can put the dates into the timeline and then have a, you know, link to that date
For whenever I'm doing my show, you know, from that page.
Like, and here it is in the timeline.
And so tomorrow I'm going to go into the timeline and condense the words down
So that, you know, it looks good.
Because it's just a still cut and paste job from Wikipedia.
And then after that, I want to go into Livy and attach the dates that are written on Livy
To my timeline, you know, and then link it back and forth with the dates here that I make.
And then that's, it'll be like ready when I do that.
It's going to be super exciting.
Okay, so I think I've talked long enough.
All right, peace out.
